OBJECTIONS TO AND MOTION TO STRIKE DECLARATION OF DOUGLAS B. BAUMGARTEN IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 55462133) FILED BY DEFENDANT BRANNAN STREET FITNESS, INC. DBA GOLD'S GYM

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT BRANNAN STREET FITNESS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED (CCP 437C). DEFENDANT HAS MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN BY DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFF ACCEPTED AN EXPRESS WAIVER AND ASSUMPTION OF RISK IN WRITING. (KNIGHT V. JEWETT (1992) 3 CAL.4TH 296, 325 (WRITTEN WORDS ARE AN EXPRESS ASSUMPTION OF RISK. SEE UMF NO. 27; WINCHESTER DECL., EXHIBIT C.) IN TURN, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT THE WAIVER SHOULD BE INVALIDATED. FIRST, THE LANGUAGE IS NEITHER UNCLEAR NOR UNAMBIGUOUS. (WINCHESTER DECL., EXHIBIT C.) SECOND, DEFENDANT DID NOT ENGAGE IN GROSS NEGLIGENCE. (CITY OF SANTA BARBARA V. SUP.CT. (2007) 41 CAL.4TH 747, 754.) PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT PROPERLY INSPECT, MAINTAIN OR KEEP MAINTENANCE RECORDS RESULTING IN GROSS NEGLIGENCE DOES NOT CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. THE OBJECTIONS TO BAUMGARTEN DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE EVIDENCE PROFFERED IN REPLY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: PAULA SHI #13358.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-05-2014 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-04-2014 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Evidence
Off Calendar May-23-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Weiss, Esq., 415.362.6765, for plaintiff Andrea Montoya.
Paul Nelson, Esq., 415.352.2700, for defendant Brannan Street Fitness, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT BRANNAN STREET FITNESS, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted (CCP 437c). Defendant has met its initial burden by demonstrating that Plaintiff accepted an express waiver and assumption of risk in writing. (Knight v. Jewett (1992) 3 Cal.4th 296, 325 (written words are an express assumption of risk. See UMF no. 27; Winchester Decl., Exhibit C.) In turn, Plaintiff has not demonstrated that the waiver should be invalidated. First, the language is neither unclear nor unambiguous. (Winchester Decl., Exhibit C.) Second, Defendant did not engage in gross negligence. (City of Santa Barbara v. Sup.Ct. (2007) 41 Cal.4th 747, 754.) Plaintiff's evidence to show that Defendant did not properly inspect, maintain or keep maintenance records resulting in gross negligence does not create a triable issue of fact. The objections to Baumgarten Declaration are sustained. The Court did not consider the evidence proffered in reply. Order signed in open court. Judgment signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Paula Shi #13358.
